reason: Layer placement: the read-only AI-callable primitive goes in tools/repoDocs.ts (pure async readRepoPlanningDocs(repoId), no onCall wrapper), GitHub access stays behind services/githubClient.ts (added listRepoDir + getRepoFile thin repos.getContent wrappers), and the only flow touch is breakdownTask.ts Step 1. Mirrors the tools/dailyIntel.ts + tools/assignTools.ts thin-tool split; no index.ts export (not a callable in W4).

Rule D best-effort everywhere: readRepoPlanningDocs NEVER throws — cache read/write failure, token-resolution failure, and GitHub fetch failure each degrade to an empty/partial result + logger.warn, so the breakdown still runs on repo-doc-only context. getRepoFile/listRepoDir normalize 404 → null/[] rather than surfacing an HttpsError.

Cache doc apps/gitsync/repos/{repoId}/meta/repoDocsCache {content, summary, source, taskCounts?, fetchedAt(ms)} — read-through with 10-min TTL + best-effort write-back, mirroring graphCache's {payload, generatedAtMs} convention. meta/ is Admin-SDK-write-only so no firestore.rules change; single-doc get/set so no composite index. Owner token resolved via repos.createdBy → users/{createdBy}.githubAccessToken; slug split on the FIRST '/' so repo names with '_' survive (prd D3).

Never log document bodies (prd security): logs carry only repoId, source, char count, taskCounts, and cache hit/miss + error strings. Used logger.info for cache hit / assembled milestones and logger.warn for every best-effort degrade.

Reused getCommitGraph's cache+slug/token resolution shape and dailyIntel's best-effort thin-tool shape rather than inventing new patterns. Octokit already a dep (no new npm dependency) — repos.getContent covers both list+file, chosen over the Git Trees API so the fetch stays path-scoped to .trellis/tasks, .claude, named root files, docs/ (structurally avoids secret paths). Char-budget heuristic (~4 chars/token, CHAR_BUDGET=32000) avoids a tokenizer dep.
